devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । मनोस्तु शतरूपायां तिस्रः कन्याश्च जज्ञिरे । आकूतिर्देवहूतिश्च प्रसूतिरिति विश्रुताः ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca manostu śatarūpāyāṃ tisraḥ kanyāśca jajñire ākūtirdevahūtiśca prasūtiriti viśrutāḥ

translation:
Maitreya said:
Besides (his two sons Priyavrata and Uttānapāda) Manu had three daughters from Śatarūpā. They became well known as Ākūti, Devahūti and Prasūti.

devanagari: आकूतिं रुचये प्रादाद् अपि भ्रातृमतीं नृपः । पुत्रिकाधर्ममाश्रित्य शतरूपानुमोदितः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: ākūtiṃ rucaye prādād api bhrātṛmatīṃ nṛpaḥ putrikādharmamāśritya śatarūpānumoditaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ prā class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ prā class 2 verb]

translation: The king, with the consent of Śatarūpā, gave Akūti to (the progenitor) Ruci in marriage, on condition of Putrikā dharma[1] (even though) Akūti had brothers.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Putrikā-dharma: When a daughter is given in marriage on the explicit condition that the first male child of the daughter shall be the (adopted) son of the giver, it is called Putrikā dharma. This was done by one who had only daughters and no son. The obvious object was securing the continuity of one’s race. Padaratnāvalī explains that although Manu had sons, he laid down this condition as he knew that Lord Viṣṇu would be born to Akūti. He wished to secure Viṣṇu’s higher grace by becoming his adopted father.

devanagari: प्रजापतिः स भगवान् रुचिस्तस्यां अजीजनत् । मिथुनं ब्रह्मवर्चस्वी परमेण समाधिना ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: prajāpatiḥ sa bhagavān rucistasyāṃ ajījanat mithunaṃ brahmavarcasvī parameṇa samādhinā
translation: The venerable progenitor Ruci, full of the spiritual glory of Brahman, begot through her a twin—a son and a daughter, by virtue of his supreme concentration (of mind on the Lord).

devanagari: यस्तयोः पुरुषः साक्षात् विष्णुर्यज्ञस्वरूपधृक् । या स्त्री सा दक्षिणा भूतेः अंशभूतानपायिनी ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: yastayoḥ puruṣaḥ sākṣāt viṣṇuryajñasvarūpadhṛk yā strī sā dakṣiṇā bhūteḥ aṃśabhūtānapāyinī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [locative dual from √ yas class 4 verb]
[genitive dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [locative dual from √ yas class 4 verb]
[genitive dual from √ yas class 4 verb], [locative dual from √ yas class 4 verb]

translation: Of the two, the male child was Viṣṇu himself who had assumed the form of Yajña). And the female child was called Dakṣiṇā (‘sacrificial fee’) who was a part of Lakṣmī (the goddess of fortune) inseparable (from Viṣṇu)[2].
purport:
Footnote [2]:
anapāyinī: Bhāvāratha Dīpikā, Bhāgavata Candrikā VC. point out that Dakṣiṇā was an aṃśa (part) of Lakṣmī. Hence the marriage of Yajña and Dakṣiṇā, though born as brother and sister, is not objectionable. Padaratnāvalī takes the ward to mean ‘One who has neither birth nor death’. Like Nārāyaṇa, Lakṣmī manifested herself to others as an āvirbhāva.

devanagari: आनिन्ये स्वगृहं पुत्र्याः पुत्रं विततरोचिषम् । स्वायम्भुवो मुदा युक्तो रुचिर्जग्राह दक्षिणाम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: āninye svagṛhaṃ putryāḥ putraṃ vitatarociṣam svāyambhuvo mudā yukto rucirjagrāha dakṣiṇām

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: (As per stipulation in the marriage) Svāyambhuva Manu brought to his house his daughter’s son of pervasive glory. With great delight, Ruci (also) joyfully accepted Dakṣiṇā (as his child).

devanagari: तां कामयानां भगवान् उवाह यजुषां पतिः । तुष्टायां तोषमापन्नोऽ जनयद् द्वादशात्मजान् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: tāṃ kāmayānāṃ bhagavān uvāha yajuṣāṃ patiḥ tuṣṭāyāṃ toṣamāpanno

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

[nominative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [vocative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [accusative single from √ jan class 10 verb], [nominative single from √ jan ], [vocative single from √ jan ], [accusative single from √ jan ]

translation: Lord Yajña, the Lord of all Sacrifices (or mantras) married hen (Dakṣiṇā) who was in love with him. He felt delighted and had twelve sons by her, who too was highly pleased.

devanagari: तोषः प्रतोषः सन्तोषो भद्रः शान्तिरिडस्पतिः । इध्मः कविर्विभुः स्वह्नः सुदेवो रोचनो द्विषट् ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: toṣaḥ pratoṣaḥ santoṣo bhadraḥ śāntiriḍaspatiḥ idhmaḥ kavirvibhuḥ svahnaḥ sudevo rocano dviṣaṭ
translation: These twelve sons were: Toṣa, Pratoṣa, Santoṣa, Bhadra, Śānti, Iḍaspati, Idhma, Kavi, Vibhu, Svahna, Sudeva, Rocana.

devanagari: तुषिता नाम ते देवा आसन् स्वायम्भुवान्तरे । मरीचिमिश्रा ऋषयो यज्ञः सुरगणेश्वरः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tuṣitā nāma te devā āsan svāyambhuvāntare marīcimiśrā ṛṣayo yajñaḥ suragaṇeśvaraḥ
translation: In the epoch (Manvantara) called Svāyambhuva, they (these sons of Yajña and Dakṣiṇā) officiated as gods, and were (collectively) called Tuṣitas; Marīci and others were the seven sages (Saptarṣis of that era) while Yajña, the incarnation of Hari, occupied the post of Indra (the rulership of gods).

devanagari: प्रियव्रतोत्तानपादौ मनुपुत्रौ महौजसौ । तत्पुत्रपौत्रनप्तॄणां अनुवृत्तं तदन्तरम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: priyavratottānapādau manuputrau mahaujasau tatputrapautranaptṝṇāṃ anuvṛttaṃ tadantaram
translation: Priyavrata and Uttānapāda, the sons of Manu, were endowed with great power and splendour. They, their sons, grandsons (both from sons and daughters) and their descendants ruled throughout the period (of this Manvantara)[3].
purport:
Footnote [3]:
According to Viṣṇu Purāṇa 3.2.48 every Manvantara (epoch of a manu) has the following batch of office bearers: (1) Manu, (2) seven sages—Saptaṛṣis, (3) gods, (4) The ruler of gods—Indra, (5) Ruling progeny of Manu. To these Bhāgavata Purāṇa 12.7.15 adds: (6) Incarnations of Hari. The dignitaries of these posts in the Svāyambhuva Manvantara are mentioned in the above verses.

devanagari: देवहूतिमदात् तात कर्दमायात्मजां मनुः । तत्संबन्धि श्रुतप्रायं भवता गदतो मम ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: devahūtimadāt tāta kardamāyātmajāṃ manuḥ tatsaṃbandhi śrutaprāyaṃ bhavatā gadato mama

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperfect active third single], [aorist active third single]

translation: Oh Vidura, Manu gave his (second) daughter Devahūti to Kardama (a Lord of created beings). (History) relating to them has been already heard' by you as narrated by me.

devanagari: दक्षाय ब्रह्मपुत्राय प्रसूतिं भगवान्मनुः । प्रायच्छद्यत्कृतः सर्गः त्रिलोक्यां विततो महान् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: dakṣāya brahmaputrāya prasūtiṃ bhagavānmanuḥ prāyacchadyatkṛtaḥ sargaḥ trilokyāṃ vitato mahān

translation: Venerable Manu gave (his third daughter) Prasūti (in marriage) to Dakṣa (another Lord of creation), a mind- born son of Brahmā. Their progeny has very widely spread all over the three worlds.

devanagari: याः कर्दमसुताः प्रोक्ता नव ब्रह्मर्षिपत्‍नयः । तासां प्रसूतिप्रसवं प्रोच्यमानं निबोध मे ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: yāḥ kardamasutāḥ proktā nava brahmarṣipat‍nayaḥ tāsāṃ prasūtiprasavaṃ procyamānaṃ nibodha me

translation: Now, hear as I tell you about the sons and grandsons of the nine daughters of Karḍama who became the wives of the Brāhmaṇa Sages (born of Brahmā) and about whom I have spoken to you.

devanagari: पत्‍नी मरीचेस्तु कला सुषुवे कर्दमात्मजा । कश्यपं पूर्णिमानं च ययोः आपूरितं जगत् ॥ १३ ॥

translation: Kalā, the daughter of Kardama and the wife of the sage Marīci, gave birth to two sons—Kaśyapa and Pūrṇiman whose descendants have fully populated (filled) the world.

devanagari: पूर्णिमासूत विरजं विश्वगं च परन्तप । देवकुल्यां हरेः पाद शौचाद्याभूत्सरिद्दिवः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: pūrṇimāsūta virajaṃ viśvagaṃ ca parantapa devakulyāṃ hareḥ pāda śaucādyābhūtsariddivaḥ
translation: Oh conqueror of enemies (Vidura), Pūrṇiman was the father of (sons) Viraja and Viśvaga and (a daughter called) Devakulyā who washing the feet of Hari became the heavenly river Gaṅgā (in her next birth).

devanagari: अत्रेः पत्‍न्यनसूया त्रीन् जज्ञे सुयशसः सुतान् । दत्तं दुर्वाससं सोमं आत्मेशब्रह्मसम्भवान् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: atreḥ pat‍nyanasūyā trīn jajñe suyaśasaḥ sutān dattaṃ durvāsasaṃ somaṃ ātmeśabrahmasambhavān

translation: Anasūyā, the wife of Atri, gave birth to three illustrious sons, viz. Datta (god Dattātreya, the sage), Durvāsas and the Moon-god, each of whom was separately born (as a manifestation of the portion) of Viṣṇu, Rudra and Brahma (respectively).

devanagari: विदुर उवाच । अत्रेर्गृहे सुरश्रेष्ठाः स्थित्युत्पत्त्यन्तहेतवः । किञ्चित् चिकीर्षवो जाता एतदाख्याहि मे गुरो ॥ १६ ॥

translation:
Vidura said:
Oh Preceptor, please narrate to me for what purpose these (three) most eminent gods who are severally the cause of the creation, preservation and destruction (of the world), were born in the house of (the sage) Atri.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । ब्रह्मणा चोदितः सृष्टौ अत्रिर्ब्रह्मविदां वरः । सह पत्‍न्या ययावृक्षं कुलाद्रिं तपसि स्थितः ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca brahmaṇā coditaḥ sṛṣṭau atrirbrahmavidāṃ varaḥ saha pat‍nyā yayāvṛkṣaṃ kulādriṃ tapasi sthitaḥ

[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
When the sage Atri, the foremost of the knowers of Brahman, was urged by god Brahmā (to undertake the work of) creation, he along with his wife went to one of the principal mountain ranges, Ṛkṣa[4] and was engaged in the performance of austere penance.
purport:
Footnote [4]:
Ṛkṣa—The eastern part of the Vindhya range extending from the Bay of Bengal to the sources of the Narmadā and the Śoṇa—including the mountains of Chhota Nagpur and Gondwana in which the Mahānadī rises—GDAMI 168-69.

devanagari: तस्मिन् प्रसूनस्तबक पलाशाशोककानने । वार्भिः स्रवद्भिरुद्‍घुष्टे निर्विन्ध्यायाः समन्ततः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin prasūnastabaka palāśāśokakānane vārbhiḥ sravadbhirud‍ghuṣṭe nirvindhyāyāḥ samantataḥ
translation: The mountain was covered with the forest of Palāśa and Aśoka trees which were beautified with clusters of flowers. All around, it was resounded with the echoes of the waterfalls of the river Nirvindhyā. On that mountain, the sage controlled his mind by means of Prāṇāyāma (breathcontrol) and stood for one hundred years on one leg, defying heat and cold (and such other pairs of opposites), and subsisted on air.

devanagari: प्राणायामेन संयम्य मनो वर्षशतं मुनिः । अतिष्ठत् एकपादेन निर्द्वन्द्वोऽनिलभोजनः ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: prāṇāyāmena saṃyamya mano varṣaśataṃ muniḥ atiṣṭhat ekapādena nirdvandvo
translation: The mountain was covered with the forest of Palāśa and Aśoka trees which were beautified with clusters of flowers. All around, it was resounded with the echoes of the waterfalls of the river Nirvindhyā. On that mountain, the sage controlled his mind by means of Prāṇāyāma (breathcontrol) and stood for one hundred years on one leg, defying heat and cold (and such other pairs of opposites), and subsisted on air.

devanagari: शरणं तं प्रपद्येऽहं य एव जगदीश्वरः । प्रजां आत्मसमां मह्यं प्रयच्छत्विति चिन्तयन् ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: śaraṇaṃ taṃ prapadye prajāṃ ātmasamāṃ mahyaṃ prayacchatviti cintayan

translation: He contemplated (and prayed in his mind): ‘I take shelter under him who alone is the Lord of the World. May he bless me with progeny just like himself.’

devanagari: तप्यमानं त्रिभुवनं प्राणायामैधसाग्निना । निर्गतेन मुनेर्मूर्ध्नः समीक्ष्य प्रभवस्त्रयः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: tapyamānaṃ tribhuvanaṃ prāṇāyāmaidhasāgninā nirgatena munermūrdhnaḥ samīkṣya prabhavastrayaḥ

[nominative single from √ tap class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ tap class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ tap class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ tap class 4 verb]

translation: Seeing that the three worlds were being distressed by the fire produced by the fuel of (the sage’s) breathcontrol (Prāṇāyāma) and issuing from the head of the sage, the three Lords (of the world, Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva) went to the hermitage of the sage—the three Gods whose glories were extensively eulogised by celestial damsels, sages, Gandharvas (celestial musicians), and (demigods like) Siddhas, Vidyādharas and Nāgas.

devanagari: अप्सरोमुनिगन्धर्व सिद्धविद्याधरोरगैः । वितायमानयशसः तदा आश्रमपदं ययुः ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: apsaromunigandharva siddhavidyādharoragaiḥ vitāyamānayaśasaḥ tadā āśramapadaṃ yayuḥ
translation: Seeing that the three worlds were being distressed by the fire produced by the fuel of (the sage’s) breathcontrol (Prāṇāyāma) and issuing from the head of the sage, the three Lords (of the world, Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva) went to the hermitage of the sage—the three Gods whose glories were extensively eulogised by celestial damsels, sages, Gandharvas (celestial musicians), and (demigods like) Siddhas, Vidyādharas and Nāgas.

devanagari: तत्प्रादुर्भावसंयोग विद्योतितमना मुनिः । उत्तिष्ठन्नेकपादेन ददर्श विबुधर्षभान् ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tatprādurbhāvasaṃyoga vidyotitamanā muniḥ uttiṣṭhannekapādena dadarśa vibudharṣabhān

translation: The sage got his mind illuminated by the simultaneous manifestation of the three gods. (To show them respect) he stretched up his body (while standing on the leg), and saw those prominent gods.

devanagari: प्रणम्य दण्डवद्‍भूमौ उपतस्थेऽर्हणाञ्जलिः । वृषहंससुपर्णस्थान् स्वैः स्वैश्चिह्नैश्च चिह्नितान् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: praṇamya daṇḍavad‍bhūmau upatasthe vṛṣahaṃsasuparṇasthān svaiḥ svaiścihnaiśca cihnitān

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: (He paid them homage) by prostrating himself fully (before them) on the ground, like a stick. With articles of worship in the hollow of his palms, he respectfully received the gods who were severally riding a swan, Garuḍa and a bull and were characterised by their speciality (viz. possession of a Kamaṇḍalu, Sudarśana discus and a trident).

devanagari: कृपावलोकेन हसद् वदनेनोपलम्भितान् । तद् रोचिषा प्रतिहते निमील्य मुनिरक्षिणी ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: kṛpāvalokena hasad vadanenopalambhitān tad rociṣā pratihate nimīlya munirakṣiṇī

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ has class 1 verb], [vocative single from √ has class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ has class 1 verb]

translation: They directly expressed their pleasure by their gracious looks and smiling faces. The sage closed his eyes as they were dazzled by their (gods’) brilliance.

devanagari: चेतस्तत्प्रवणं युञ्जन् अस्तावीत्संहताञ्जलिः । श्लक्ष्णया सूक्तया वाचा सर्वलोकगरीयसः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: cetastatpravaṇaṃ yuñjan astāvītsaṃhatāñjaliḥ ślakṣṇayā sūktayā vācā sarvalokagarīyasaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ yuj class 7 verb], [vocative single from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: With his mind concentrated on them and with folded palms he praised, in sweet words of deep significance, the three gods who are the supreme-most in all the worlds.

devanagari: अत्रिरुवाच । विश्वोद्‍भवस्थितिलयेषु विभज्यमानैः । मायागुणैरनुयुगं विगृहीतदेहाः । ते ब्रह्मविष्णुगिरिशाः प्रणतोऽस्म्यहं वः । तेभ्यः क एव ...
english_devnagari: atriruvāca viśvod‍bhavasthitilayeṣu vibhajyamānaiḥ māyāguṇairanuyugaṃ vigṛhītadehāḥ te brahmaviṣṇugiriśāḥ praṇato tebhyaḥ ka eva bhavatāṃ me ihopahūtaḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Atri said:
You are (obviously the celebrated gods) Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva who in every Yuga (kalpa), assume forms with the help of the guṇas (attributes) of Māyā, trifurcated for bringing about the creation, maintenance and dissolution of the universe. I bow to you all. (Kindly tell me) which of you has been invocated by me here.

devanagari: एको मयेह भगवान् विविधप्रधानैः । चित्तीकृतः प्रजननाय कथं नु यूयम् । अत्रागतास्तनुभृतां मनसोऽपि दूराद् । ब्रूत प्रसीदत महानिह विस्मयो मे ॥...
english_devnagari: eko mayeha bhagavān vividhapradhānaiḥ cittīkṛtaḥ prajananāya kathaṃ nu yūyam atrāgatāstanubhṛtāṃ manaso brūta prasīdata mahāniha vismayo me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative active second single]
[present middle first single], [imperative active second single]

translation: In this hermitage, I have concentrated (contemplated) only on one glorious Lord by various means, with a desire to get a child. It is a great surprise to me how (all of) you who are beyond the range of the mind of bodied beings, have come here. Be pleased to tell me the mystery.

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इति तस्य वचः श्रुत्वा त्रयस्ते विबुधर्षभाः । प्रत्याहुः श्लक्ष्णया वाचा प्रहस्य तं ऋषिं प्रभो ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvāca iti tasya vacaḥ śrutvā trayaste vibudharṣabhāḥ pratyāhuḥ ślakṣṇayā vācā prahasya taṃ ṛṣiṃ prabho

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation:
Maitreya said:
Oh Lord (Vidura), hearing this speech of the sage (Atri), the three prominent gods laughed. They replied to the sage in soft gentle words (as follows):

devanagari: देवा ऊचुः । यथा कृतस्ते सङ्कल्पो भाव्यं तेनैव नान्यथा । सत्सङ्कल्पस्य ते ब्रह्मन् यद्वै ध्यायति ते वयम् ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: devā ūcuḥ yathā kṛtaste saṅkalpo bhāvyaṃ tenaiva nānyathā satsaṅkalpasya te brahman yadvai dhyāyati te vayam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ kṛ class 1 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 5 verb], [nominative single from √ kṛ class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ bhū ], [accusative single from √ bhū ]

translation:
The gods said:
“Oh Brahman! As you have willed it, so shall it exactly happen and not otherwise. You are of true resolve. We three form that Principle (called rhe Lord of the world) which you are contemplating.

devanagari: अथास्मद् अंशभूतास्ते आत्मजा लोकविश्रुताः । भवितारोऽङ्ग भद्रं ते विस्रप्स्यन्ति च ते यशः ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: athāsmad aṃśabhūtāste ātmajā lokaviśrutāḥ bhavitāro
translation: Oh sage! May you be blessed! from our aṃśa (parts) now you will beget sons who (themselves) will be celebrated in the world and spread your reputation as well.”

devanagari: एवं कामवरं दत्त्वा प्रतिजग्मुः सुरेश्वराः । सभाजितास्तयोः सम्यग् दम्पत्योर्मिषतोस्ततः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ kāmavaraṃ dattvā pratijagmuḥ sureśvarāḥ sabhājitāstayoḥ samyag dampatyormiṣatostataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]
[nominative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [vocative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb], [accusative plural from √ sabhāj class 10 verb]
[genitive dual from √ miṣ class 6 verb], [locative dual from √ miṣ class 6 verb]
[genitive dual from √ miṣ class 6 verb], [locative dual from √ miṣ class 6 verb]

translation: Having granted their desired boon, the Lords of gods who were properly worshipped by the couple, returned from that hermitage while they (Atri and Anasūyā) stood looking on (agape).

devanagari: सोमोऽभूद्‍ब्रह्मणोंऽशेन दत्तो विष्णोस्तु योगवित् । दुर्वासाः शंकरस्यांशो निबोधाङ्गिरसः प्रजाः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: somo durvāsāḥ śaṃkarasyāṃśo nibodhāṅgirasaḥ prajāḥ
translation: Thus Soma was born with a portion of Brahmā, Datta, the master of Yoga, with that of Viṣṇu, while Durvāsas with the portion of Śaṅkara. Now listen (to me), about the progeny of Aṅgiras (another mind-born son of Brahmā).

devanagari: श्रद्धा त्वङ्गिरसः पत्‍नी चतस्रोऽसूत कन्यकाः । सिनीवाली कुहू राका चतुर्थ्यनुमतिस्तथा ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: śraddhā tvaṅgirasaḥ pat‍nī catasro sinīvālī kuhū rākā caturthyanumatistathā
translation: Śraddhā, the wife of Aṅgiras gave birth to four daughters viz. Sinīvālī, Kuhū, Rākā and the fourth Anumati.[5]
purport:
Footnote [5]:
These are the deities presiding over the 14th and 15th day of the bright and dark fortnights who came to designate those days. Thus Sinīvālī is the day preceding the new moon day. On this day the moon rises with a scarcely visible crescent, while Kuhū is the 15th day or the last day of the lunar month when the moon is invisible. Rākā presides over the full moon (15th) day, while Anumati is the 14th day in the bright half—Vide ASD. under these heads.

devanagari: तत्पुत्रावपरावास्तां ख्यातौ स्वारोचिषेऽन्तरे । उतथ्यो भगवान् साक्षात् ब्रह्मिष्ठश्च बृहस्पतिः ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: tatputrāvaparāvāstāṃ khyātau svārociṣe utathyo bhagavān sākṣāt brahmiṣṭhaśca bṛhaspatiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative dual from √ khyā class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ khyā class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ khyā class 2 verb]

translation: Besides these he (Aṅgiras) had two sons—Utathya who was the venerable Lord himself and Bṛhaspati (the preceptor of gods) the eminent knower of Brahman. They became celebrities in the Svārociṣa Manvantara.

devanagari: पुलस्त्योऽजनयत्पत्‍न्यां अगस्त्यं च हविर्भुवि । सोऽन्यजन्मनि दह्राग्निः विश्रवाश्च महातपाः ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: pulastyo so
translation: By his wife Havirbhū, Pulastya (another mind-born son of Brahmā) had a son called Agastya who was abdominal fire in another birth and also (another son) Viśravas, the great ascetic.

devanagari: तस्य यक्षपतिर्देवः कुबेरस्त्विडविडासुतः । रावणः कुम्भकर्णश्च तथान्यस्यां विभीषणः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya yakṣapatirdevaḥ kuberastviḍaviḍāsutaḥ rāvaṇaḥ kumbhakarṇaśca tathānyasyāṃ vibhīṣaṇaḥ
translation: Of Viśravas and his wife Iḍaviḍā was born god Kubera (the god of Wealth), the king of Yakṣas. Rāvaṇa, Kumbhakarṇa and Vibhīṣaṇa were born (to him) by another wife (Keśinī).

devanagari: पुलहस्य गतिर्भार्या त्रीनसूत सती सुतान् । कर्मश्रेष्ठं वरीयांसं सहिष्णुं च महामते ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: pulahasya gatirbhāryā trīnasūta satī sutān karmaśreṣṭhaṃ varīyāṃsaṃ sahiṣṇuṃ ca mahāmate
translation: Oh talented Vidura, Gati, the pious wife of Pulaha, gave birth to three sons, viz. Karmaśreṣṭha, Varīyān and Śahiṣṇu [Sahiṣṇu?].

devanagari: क्रतोरपि क्रिया भार्या वालखिल्यानसूयत । ऋषीन्षष्टिसहस्राणि ज्वलतो ब्रह्मतेजसा ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: kratorapi kriyā bhāryā vālakhilyānasūyata ṛṣīnṣaṣṭisahasrāṇi jvalato brahmatejasā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [genitive dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb], [locative dual from √ kṛ class 2 verb]

[ablative single from √ jval class 1 verb], [genitive single from √ jval class 1 verb]

translation: Even Kratu’s wife, Kriyā, bore sixty-thousand (thumb-sized) sons known as Vālakhilyas—all brilliant with the splendour of Brahman.

devanagari: ऊर्जायां जज्ञिरे पुत्रा वसिष्ठस्य परंतप । चित्रकेतुप्रधानास्ते सप्त ब्रह्मर्षयोऽमलाः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: ūrjāyāṃ jajñire putrā vasiṣṭhasya paraṃtapa citraketupradhānāste sapta brahmarṣayo
translation: Oh tormentor of enemies (Vidura), by his wife Ūrjā (Arundhatī) Vasiṣṭha had seven sons of whom Citraketu was prominent. They all were pure-hearted Brāhmaṇa-sages.

devanagari: चित्रकेतुः सुरोचिश्च विरजा मित्र एव च । उल्बणो वसुभृद्यानो द्युमान् शक्त्यादयोऽपरे ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: citraketuḥ surociśca virajā mitra eva ca ulbaṇo vasubhṛdyāno dyumān śaktyādayo
translation: They were Citraketu, Surociṣ, Virajas, Mitra, Ulbaṇa, Vasubhṛdyāna and Dyumat. He had other sons such as Śakti and others (by another wife).

devanagari: चित्तिस्त्वथर्वणः पत्‍नी लेभे पुत्रं धृतव्रतम् । दध्यञ्चमश्वशिरसं भृगोर्वंशं निबोध मे ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: cittistvatharvaṇaḥ pat‍nī lebhe putraṃ dhṛtavratam dadhyañcamaśvaśirasaṃ bhṛgorvaṃśaṃ nibodha me

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Citti (or Śānti), the wife of Atharvan, got a son, Dadhīci alias Aśvaśiras who took the vow (of austere penance). (Henceforth) hear from me the race of Bhṛgus.

devanagari: भृगुः ख्यात्यां महाभागः पत्‍न्यां पुत्रानजीजनत् । धातारं च विधातारं श्रियं च भगवत्पराम् ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: bhṛguḥ khyātyāṃ mahābhāgaḥ pat‍nyāṃ putrānajījanat dhātāraṃ ca vidhātāraṃ śriyaṃ ca bhagavatparām
translation: The distinguished sage Bhṛgu begot by his wife, Khyāti, (two) sons—Dhātṛ and Vidhātṛ, and a daughter, Śrī, who was devoted to the glorious Lord (Viṣṇu).

devanagari: आयतिं नियतिं चैव सुते मेरुस्तयोरदात् । ताभ्यां तयोरभवतां मृकण्डः प्राण एव च ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: āyatiṃ niyatiṃ caiva sute merustayoradāt tābhyāṃ tayorabhavatāṃ mṛkaṇḍaḥ prāṇa eva ca

[nominative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [locative single from √ sū class 2 verb]
[nominative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ sū class 2 verb], [vocative dual from √ sū class 2 verb], [accusative dual from √ sū class 2 verb]

translation: (The sage) Meru gave his daughters Āyati and Niyati (in marriage) to these (sons of Bhṛgu) severally. Of them (Āyati and Niyati) were born Mṛkaṇḍa and Prāṇa (respectively).

devanagari: मार्कण्डेयो मृकण्डस्य प्राणाद्वेदशिरा मुनिः । कविश्च भार्गवो यस्य भगवानुशना सुतः ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: mārkaṇḍeyo mṛkaṇḍasya prāṇādvedaśirā muniḥ kaviśca bhārgavo yasya bhagavānuśanā sutaḥ
translation: Of Mṛkaṇḍa was born Mārkaṇḍeya, and of Prāṇa, the sage Vedaśiras. Kavi, (another) son of Bhṛgu, had the venerable Uśanas (Śukrācārya, the preceptor of Daityas) as his son.

devanagari: ते एते मुनयः क्षत्तः लोकान् सर्गैरभावयन् । एष कर्दमदौहित्र सन्तानः कथितस्तव । श्रृण्वतः श्रद्दधानस्य सद्यः पापहरः परः ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: te ete munayaḥ kṣattaḥ lokān sargairabhāvayan eṣa kardamadauhitra santānaḥ kathitastava śrṛṇvataḥ śraddadhānasya sadyaḥ pāpaharaḥ paraḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [imperative active second single], [perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: All these sages peopled the worlds by their descendants. I have narrated to you, Oh Vidura, the account of the progeny of Kardama’s grandsons (from daughters’ side)—an account which immediately removes the sins of the faithful and reverential listener.

devanagari: प्रसूतिं मानवीं दक्ष उपयेमे ह्यजात्मजः । तस्यां ससर्ज दुहितॄः षोडशामललोचनाः ॥ ४७ ॥
english_devnagari: prasūtiṃ mānavīṃ dakṣa upayeme hyajātmajaḥ tasyāṃ sasarja duhitṝḥ ṣoḍaśāmalalocanāḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active second plural], [perfect active third single]

translation: Dakṣa, another (mind-born) son of god Brahmā, married Prasūti, the daughter of (Svāyambhuva) Manu[6]. He begot, by her, sixteen fair-eyed daughters.

devanagari: त्रयोदशादाद्धर्माय तथैकामग्नये विभुः । पितृभ्य एकां युक्तेभ्यो भवायैकां भवच्छिदे ॥ ४८ ॥
english_devnagari: trayodaśādāddharmāya tathaikāmagnaye vibhuḥ pitṛbhya ekāṃ yuktebhyo bhavāyaikāṃ bhavacchide

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [dative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [ablative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb]
[dative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb], [ablative plural from √ yuj class 7 verb]

translation: The Lord (Prajāpati Dakṣa) gave thirteen of his daughters to Dharma, one to Agni, one to all Pitṛs combined, and one to god Śiva, who cuts the bonds of Saṃsāra.

devanagari: श्रद्धा मैत्री दया शान्तिः तुष्टिः पुष्टिः क्रियोन्नतिः । बुद्धिर्मेधा तितिक्षा ह्रीः मूर्तिर्धर्मस्य पत्‍नयः ॥ ४९ ॥
english_devnagari: śraddhā maitrī dayā śāntiḥ tuṣṭiḥ puṣṭiḥ kriyonnatiḥ buddhirmedhā titikṣā hrīḥ mūrtirdharmasya pat‍nayaḥ
translation: [7] Śraddhā, Maitrī, Dayā, Śānti, Tuṣṭi, Puṣṭi, Kriyā, Unnati, Buddhi, Medhā, Titikṣā, Hrī and Mūrti—these (thirteen) are the wives of Dharma.

devanagari: श्रद्धासूत शुभं मैत्री प्रसादं अभयं दया । शान्तिः सुखं मुदं तुष्टिः स्मयं पुष्टिः असूयत ॥ ५० ॥
english_devnagari: śraddhāsūta śubhaṃ maitrī prasādaṃ abhayaṃ dayā śāntiḥ sukhaṃ mudaṃ tuṣṭiḥ smayaṃ puṣṭiḥ asūyata
translation:
[7] Śraddhā gave birth to Śubha (auspiciousness), Maitrī to Prasāda, Dayā to Abhaya, Śanti to Sukha, Tuṣṭi to Mud (a daughter) [Muda?] Puṣṭi to Smaya.
51-52[8]. Kriyā bore Yoga, Unnati, Darpa; Buddhi, Artha; Medhā, Smṛti (a daughter); Titikṣā, Kṣema and Hrī, a son called Praśraya. Mūrti who was the fountain-head of all excellent qualities, gave birth to sages Nara and Nārāyaṇa.
purport:
Footnote [7]:
Vide infra p. 427, note *.

Footnote [8]:
Dharma (Religion or Piety) is always associated with virtues or certain pious mental states. These are described as the wives of Dharma and his progeny from them.

devanagari: योगं क्रियोन्नतिर्दर्पं अर्थं बुद्धिरसूयत । मेधा स्मृतिं तितिक्षा तु क्षेमं ह्रीः प्रश्रयं सुतम् ॥ ५१ ॥
english_devnagari: yogaṃ kriyonnatirdarpaṃ arthaṃ buddhirasūyata medhā smṛtiṃ titikṣā tu kṣemaṃ hrīḥ praśrayaṃ sutam

devanagari: मूर्तिः सर्वगुणोत्पत्तिः नरनारायणौ ऋषी । ययोर्जन्मन्यदो विश्वं अभ्यनन्दत् सुनिर्वृतम् । मनांसि ककुभो वाताः प्रसेदुः सरितोऽद्रयः ॥ ५३ ॥

translation: When these two (sages) were born, the universe rejoiced with great pleasure. The minds (of men), the quarters, winds, rivers and mountains—all became calm and serene.

devanagari: दिव्यवाद्यन्त तूर्याणि पेतुः कुसुमवृष्टयः । मुनयस्तुष्टुवुस्तुष्टा जगुर्गन्धर्वकिन्नराः ॥ ५४ ॥

translation: Trumpets were blown in the heavens; showers of flowers rained; sages offered their prayers. Gandharvas and Kinnaras sang joyfully.

devanagari: नृत्यन्ति स्म स्त्रियो देव्य आसीत् परममङ्गलम् । देवा ब्रह्मादयः सर्वे उपतस्थुरभिष्टवैः ॥ ५५ ॥
english_devnagari: nṛtyanti sma striyo devya āsīt paramamaṅgalam devā brahmādayaḥ sarve upatasthurabhiṣṭavaiḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [vocative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb], [accusative plural from √ nṛt class 4 verb]
[aorist active third single], [injunctive active third single]

translation: The celestial nymphs and goddesses danced; highest bliss reigned everywhere. (There was auspiciousness everywhere). All divinities like Brahmā (and others) attended upon (Nara and Nārāyaṇa) with eulogistic prayers.

devanagari: देवा ऊचुः । यो मायया विरचितं निजयात्मनीदं । खे रूपभेदमिव तत्प्रतिचक्षणाय । एतेन धर्मसदने ऋषिमूर्तिनाद्य । प्रादुश्चकार पुरुषाय नमः परस्म...

translation:
Gods said:
[9] Salutations to the Supreme Man who, manifested in himself this universe created by his own Māyā power, just like the phenomenal appearance (e.g. a city of Gandharvas) in the (cloudy) sky, and who appeared today, in the house of Dharma, in the form of sages (Nara and Nārāyaṇa), for revealing the true nature of the Self.
purport:
Footnote [9]:
(i) By his Will or Māyā power, this created universe is supported on Brahman, but it does not affect the Brahman, just as colours blue, white etc. of the clouds, do not stick to the sky. Or the changes of form, such as that of a god or a subhuman being with which jīva is associated (when it is born in that particular yoni), do not affect the real nature of Ātman. The Lord has manifested himself as sages under the roof of Dharma in order to instruct the distinctness of Atman, Brahman, from the form of god, man etc., and to lead to liberation by this knowledge. We bow to that Supreme Person.—Bhāgavata Candrikā
(ii) His own Māyā has arranged (created) this universe on the support of Ātman like the cluster of clouds in the sky. For shedding light (explaining the nature) of the Ātman, he has manifested himself.—Sārārthadarśinī
(iii) Just as different, kinds of cavities, (ghaṭākāśa, maṭhākāśa) are in the sky, similarly the universe is arranged (created) in Yourself. Just as the sky is neither different from, nor circumscribed by, these cavities or vacuums, so also the Lord is neither affected nor delimited by the universe. The illustration of the sky is taken to emphasize the unattachedness and not unreality.—Bālaprabodhini
Subodhinī has not written his commentary on this skandha.

devanagari: सोऽयं स्थितिव्यतिकरोपशमाय सृष्टान् । सत्त्वेन नः सुरगणान् अनुमेयतत्त्वः । दृश्याददभ्रकरुणेन विलोकनेन । यच्छ्रीनिकेतममलं क्षिपतारविन्दम् ...
english_devnagari: so sattvena naḥ suragaṇān anumeyatattvaḥ dṛśyādadabhrakaruṇena vilokanena yacchrīniketamamalaṃ kṣipatāravindam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: May the Lord, whose real nature is to be inferred by the Śāstras, condescend to look on us, the gods, who have been created by him, by means of Sattva, for the protection of the world from the disturbances in the orderly existence of the world—his eyes which are overflowing with mercy and which surpass the beautiful lotus, receptacle of Śrī (or the home of Lakṣmī).

devanagari: एवं सुरगणैस्तात भगवन्तावभिष्टुतौ । लब्धावलोकैर्ययतुः अर्चितौ गन्धमादनम् ॥ ५८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ suragaṇaistāta bhagavantāvabhiṣṭutau labdhāvalokairyayatuḥ arcitau gandhamādanam

translation: Oh child (Vidura), the two divine sages who were thus extolled and worshipped by the (multitude of) gods, blessed them by their look, and left for mount Gandhamādana.

devanagari: तौ इमौ वै भगवतो हरेरंशाविहागतौ । भारव्ययाय च भुवः कृष्णौ यदुकुरूद्वहौ ॥ ५९ ॥
english_devnagari: tau imau vai bhagavato hareraṃśāvihāgatau bhāravyayāya ca bhuvaḥ kṛṣṇau yadukurūdvahau

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [present middle first single], [imperative middle first single]

translation: Verily, those two part (-manifestations) of Lord Hari, have incarnated in this world as Kṛṣṇa, the descendant of Yadu, and the dark-complexioned Arjuna of the Kuru-race, for relieving the burden of the earth.

devanagari: स्वाहाभिमानिनश्चाग्नेः आत्मजान् त्रीन् अजीजनत् । पावकं पवमानं च शुचिं च हुतभोजनम् ॥ ६० ॥
english_devnagari: svāhābhimāninaścāgneḥ ātmajān trīn ajījanat pāvakaṃ pavamānaṃ ca śuciṃ ca hutabhojanam

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ pav class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pav class 1 verb]
[nominative single from √ pū class 1 verb], [accusative single from √ pū class 1 verb]

translation: And Svāhā, the wife of Agni, gave birth to three sons—Pāvaka, Pavamāna and Śuci—all of whom are the deities presiding over Agni, and who subsist on sacrificial offerings.

devanagari: तेभ्योऽग्नयः समभवन् चत्वारिंशच्च पञ्च च । ते एवैकोनपञ्चाशत् साकं पितृपितामहैः ॥ ६१ ॥
english_devnagari: tebhyo te evaikonapañcāśat sākaṃ pitṛpitāmahaiḥ
translation: They gave birth to forty-five fires. These forty-five fires, their three fathers (Pāvaka, Pavamāna and Śuci) and one grandfather (god Agni) make forty-nine fire-gods.

devanagari: वैतानिके कर्मणि यत् नामभिर्ब्रह्मवादिभिः । आग्नेय्य इष्टयो यज्ञे निरूप्यन्तेऽग्नयस्तु ते ॥ ६२ ॥
english_devnagari: vaitānike karmaṇi yat nāmabhirbrahmavādibhiḥ āgneyya iṣṭayo yajñe nirūpyante

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [nominative single from √ i class 2 verb], [vocative single from √ i class 2 verb], [accusative single from √ i class 2 verb]

translation: These are the fire-gods in whose name iṣṭis (small sacrifices) called Āgneya (pertaining to Agni) are performed during Vedic sacrificial sessions, by persons, well-versed in the Vedas.

devanagari: अग्निष्वात्ता बर्हिषदः सौम्याः पितर आज्यपाः । साग्नयोऽनग्नयस्तेषां पत्‍नी दाक्षायणी स्वधा ॥ ६३ ॥
english_devnagari: agniṣvāttā barhiṣadaḥ saumyāḥ pitara ājyapāḥ sāgnayo
translation: The manes (Pitṛs) are[10] (1) Agniṣvātta,(2) Barhiṣad, (3) Saumya and (4) Ājyapa. They are either Sāgnaya (receiving libations of water through the sacred fire) or Anagnaya (receiving libations directly without agnaukaraṇa). Svadhā, the daughter of Dakṣa was their wife.
purport:
Footnote [10]:
These are main divisions of the Pitṛs: (1) Those who joined the manes by following only Smārta karmas are Agniṣvātta; (2) Those who joined forefathers after performing Agnihotra and Vedic sacrifices are Barhiṣadas; (3) Those who drink Soma in sacrifices and become Pitṛs are Somapas or Saumyas; and (4) Those who partake of ghee in sacrifices are ājyapa.

devanagari: तेभ्यो दधार कन्ये द्वे वयुनां धारिणीं स्वधा । उभे ते ब्रह्मवादिन्यौ ज्ञानविज्ञानपारगे ॥ ६४ ॥
english_devnagari: tebhyo dadhāra kanye dve vayunāṃ dhāriṇīṃ svadhā ubhe te brahmavādinyau jñānavijñānapārage

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]
[perfect active first single], [perfect active third single]

translation: Svadhā bore to them two daughters—Vayunā and Dhāriṇī. Both of them were well-versed in scriptural knowledge and spiritual knowledge, and they expounded the same.

devanagari: भवस्य पत्‍नी तु सती भवं देवमनुव्रता । आत्मनः सदृशं पुत्रं न लेभे गुणशीलतः ॥ ६५ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavasya pat‍nī tu satī bhavaṃ devamanuvratā ātmanaḥ sadṛśaṃ putraṃ na lebhe guṇaśīlataḥ

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [perfect middle first single], [perfect middle third single]

translation: Though Satī, the wife of Śiva who was like him in qualities and character served him, she had no son.

devanagari: पितर्यप्रतिरूपे स्वे भवायानागसे रुषा । अप्रौढैवात्मनात्मानं अजहाद् योगसंयुता ॥ ६६ ॥
english_devnagari: pitaryapratirūpe sve bhavāyānāgase ruṣā aprauḍhaivātmanātmānaṃ ajahād yogasaṃyutā

analyses.morphological_and_syntactical_analysis: [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]
[nominative single from √ man class 4 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 4 verb], [nominative single from √ man class 8 verb], [accusative single from √ man class 8 verb]

translation:
(For) when she was quite young, she cast off her body of her own accord by Yogic process, as she was angry with her father who was antagonistic to her innocent husband, Śiva.
